name: Chang'e 3 / Yutu
fact: China's first lunar landing — the first soft landing anywhere on the Moon in 37 years. The Chang'e 3 lander is still alive, making it the longest-surviving lunar lander ever. Yutu drove 114 m before its drive mechanism failed, but its instruments continued returning science data.
capability: Re-established robotic landing capability after a decades-long pause — the foundation for the sample-return missions (Chang'e 5, 6) that informed Mars Sample Return architecture.
